Works fantastically.  I use this exclusively over the oven.  I notice people always complain about these taking forever to heat up.  The secret is to use cast iron with them.  If you are using aluminum pans, for instance, it will take a while because the specific gravity is higher than cast iron.  Most people who buy these did not pay attention in 5th grade science class.  If you want to be an energy hog go right ahead.  These things are fantastic.  Buy a cast iron skillet and pan if you are going to use this.  Otherwise, don't complain that your inferior pots and pans will not heat up.

The actual whirring sound while the machine is making ice is not too loud in my opinion. On par with the quieter dishwasher models. However, the ice falls into the bucket with a bang. It doesn't bother me much, but something to think about.
All in all, this machine does what I needed it to do, and it does it well. Depending on what you are looking for in an icemaker, it's a very solid option and I'm pleased with my purchase.

Be aware, we ran into a problem replacing our old 760 model with the 700. The problem was the old controller is inside the house on our wall. With the 700 digital, The controller has to be installed on the humidifier unit (thus for our unit, the controller would be in the crawl space under the house- not ideal!). You can't just replace the digital controller with the old controller inside the house. This you can't control the digital from inside your house. You have to get 700 model with manual control. The manual control can replace the 760 control inside the house.

First, the good points:

- If the most important thing about a dishwasher is that it gets dishes clean without a lot of pre-scrubbing on your part, this machine excels. The pots & pans cycle, which I find myself using most of the time (though it takes an hour and 40 minutes, 15 minutes longer than the standard cycle) does indeed scrub off all but the most stubborn baked-on gunk. It provides a good hot-water rinse to sterilize the dishes, and efficiently dries everything but plastic items like tupperware. Using a quality detergent is key: I use Cascade gel. I've found it's essential to use rinse aid if you want the dishes dried thoroughly; I use Jet-Dry. The other, shorter cycles -- for stemware and the like -- don't seem to do as good a job either at cleaning or drying the dishes. The 10-minute rinse & hold cycle is useful as a pre-wash, and to bring hot water through the pipes in the house so the regular cycle starts with hot water.

- Noise level -- it's very quiet.

- Looks -- It really is a beautiful machine.

- Price -- cheaper than most good standard-size machines, and far less than other odd-size dishwashers (like narrow units).

Now, the shortcomings:

- The maximum diameter of plates and lids that fit efficiently, so they stand up straight, is 9". Is your dinnerware only 9" wide? My plates are 10-1/2", and they have to be angled so severely that it dramatically reduces the number of items I can put in. I shouldn't have to go out and buy all new dinnerware just to accommodate my tiny dishwasher.

- The tines that hold things in place are all wrong: most of my glassware doesn't easily fit without tipping over, and there's a weird gap in the tines that's only good for a small piece of bakeware -- and even that slides around. Tines that allow you to fold them down or adjust their angle would have been nice.

Our speed queen dryer has a longer exhaust duct than this part is designed to handle.  Because of the pressure resistance, the heating coil burns open.  The first one opened under warranty.  The second time was after the warranty expired and the part and service call set us back over $200.  You can purchase this part, then take the thermostat, the thermal switch and wire jumpers off of your existing part and place them on this part.  Replacing it takes about 10 minutes.  You can see how via Youtube.  I gave it 4 stars because the hole stamps for one of the thermal switches was just slightly off.  The part seems to work fine.  BTW, watch the heater as you start your dryer.  It gets red hot!!  I should also add that for the prior 15 years, our other dryers never had a heating element burn out.

I am 5'6" and I'm able to wash a pair of jeans with two t-shirts or four shirts. I follow the instructions and fill about three inches of water and run with a small amount of detergent for three minutes to dissolve the detergent. Then I load my clothes.

I run a 15 minute cycle. I drain and rinse, and then refill the washer to put it through it's "rinse" cycle for another six to nine minutes. I rinse and squeeze out.

I run two batches like the above. The only complaint I have is I don't think the motor will last as you can smell it on the second round. But I have to re-read the instructions on how to maintain the machine. I also wished they have where to fill with water. I marked the cut off with tape.

I can store everything in a cabinet. I'm suggesting this to friends who own condos and don't have a washer and dryer hookup or have a building laundromat that constantly is broken or floods.

I bought this for my RV, which I live in full-time. I do have full hookups and could have installed a washer dryer combo or stackable, but I preferred to keep some extra closet space and also keep the weight down.

I researched and debated on these small machines forever, and I'm happy to say this one is an excellent choice. I bought a y-splitter to connect this to both the hot and cold hookups in the RV.

The machine is fully automatic so it runs just like a normal washing machine. It fits much more than I thought. I can actually wash a king size sheet and all four pillowcases, then I wash the other sheet separately with towels. It will fit my heavy bib overalls plus a few work shirts (I work in construction).

I hang dry my laundry, which is a bit more labor than using a dryer but helps clothes last longer, and still beats hauling laundry to the laundry facility during my limited free time.

I can 100% recommend this machine. I've been using it regularly for probably 8 months now. Sometimes I slack on keeping up with the wash and end up running four loads in a row, and it's holding up great and sounds as good as new. It's only ever gone off balance one time, this was with too many towels.

I'd recommend a drain pan to go under it if you're using it in a carpeted RV closet like I am. It's never leaked but still worth doing for peace of mind. One other RV-specific tip: save the Styrofoam shipping block that comes in the machine. Use that when towing the RV to prevent abusing the drum when bouncing down the road.

If you accidently make it to weak add back what you first poured out the jar that brought it from full to 5 cups.

I'm giving this 2 stars instead of 1 because I did get it to work, how long it lasts is yet to be seen. In spite of this supposedly being a direct replacement for my LG front load washer's cold water valve (AP5986564), It DOES NOT have a 3/4 male hose thread connection like the old one. The threads are very close to 3/4 MPT and I was able to use a brass 3/4 female pipe thread to 3/4 male hose thread adapter to get where I needed to be. Be sure to use some thread tape to help seal it up. It pushes the hose connection closer to the wall, but it wasn't a problem. The rest of  the valve was close enough to work. The mounting plate was compatible. As you can see in the picture, the solenoid orientation was a little different, but I was able to still connect the wiring. The adapter almost doubles the cost of the part, even more if you consider your time and gas to go to the hardware store.  All in all, I would not recommend this part, spend a little more and get one that actually has the proper threads

I'm not sure what I think of this yet. I got it and it arrived exactly when it was supposed to. I opened the box and it's lightweight making it very portable. I set it on my kitchen counter so it could drain into the sink. The output hose hooks on to the side of the unit which is a great feature so when the washing machine is moved it's not in the way. The electrical cord is long so it's easy to find a outlet that it will reach to. The instructions a horribly written making it very difficult to figure out how to use this thing. I finally ended up thinking I was going to apply the way I use a full sized washing machine to this and hope for the best. The thing to remember is this is a semi automatic washer so it doesn't have all the features of a full sized machine. Upon opening the lid I noticed a very strong plastic smell, so I ran the unit with soap and without clothes which helped to get rid of the plastic smell. The first time I put clothes in it and washed them I used a tablespoon of laundry detergent. This was not enough. The second time I used it I used 2 tablespoons and it was much better. I'm not sure how liquid laundry detergent would be. Maybe 1 tablespoon would be enough. I didn't see anything mentioned in the instructions about a lint trap, so I threw in two small ones I had and let them float on top of the wash and they seemed to do well. The machine itself agitates very well and it does a good job of getting clothes clean. Would I say it's as good as a full sized machine, no. I did one wash cycle and two rinse cycles with it. It doesn't have a rinse cycle, but I ran it twice on the wash cycle without any soap and this worked well. My only other complaint than the horrendous instructions is that 3 different models of wahsing machines all with different functions are listed together. It gets too confusing what washing machine does what. The one I bought was the wrong one and not the one It seems others are also confused by the different models and which one does what as it's not clearly stated. I will only be using this for about 6 months and then I will be getting a new full sized washing machine. It does the job, but I can't say it's something I would want to use forever. I was in a pinch and had to make a decision.

This is an AMAZING gas range - best one I've ever owned.  The features are exactly what I wanted - no compromising for a change:  huge oven, convection, cleaning feature, full grated top with 5 burners, black porcelain seamless top.  I chose this model because it was in Consumer Reports top 5 free-standing gas ranges.  Samsung made 4 of the 5 (the GE was way overpriced) and this was the only one that had the black top - the others had shiny stainless steel tops.  From years of cooking experience, I knew that the shiny stainless steel would be a nightmare to keep looking clean.  I did not need the warming drawer - had one once and never used it.  This oven does have a keep warm feature anyway.  The storage drawer is smaller than most stoves, but the trade off is that the oven is much larger and since I cook big meals often, this is a great feature.  The ONLY thing I'm a little disappointed about is the fact that it only has 2 oven racks, instead of 3, but I do have a freestanding rack with 1" feet that I use as the bottom rack when needed. Read directions; followed to the letter, took 2 hours to flush out system by dispensing water every 30 seconds on and 60 seconds off as directed by instructions.

When finished, I dispensed a glass of water to drink. What hit me first was the CHEMICAL SMELL, it was overwhelming. I took a small taste of it, ugh! It was SO FOUL that I spit it in my sink without swallowing it. The water had a CHEMICAL TASTE that was horrendous.

Thinking that I had gotten a defective water filter, I installed the 2nd one in the bundle. Same result, foul tasting and smelling water. I thought maybe if I flushed it some more than it would okay, so I flushed 4 gallons through fridge, the taste and smell NEVER went away.

Promptly ordered an OEM part and installed it; my water tastes great again! I will always buy an LG part for my refrigerator going forward and will never trust aftermarket again. BUYER BEWARE.

Update: Changed rating from 2 to 4 stars. After several washings, the performance has improved. I recommend still using the liquid humidifier treatment that makes the mineral buildup more soluble and easier to wash out. A final rinse in ice cold water seems to help.

Using with Vornado EVAP40 4 gallon humidifier, with only moderately hard water, the filters need to be turned after about 2 gallons. At 3 gallons, evaporation slows down to less than half the original rate. This is not due to humidity already present. My humidity is kept relatively constant. What happens is that by the time most of the water is evaporated, only the bottom third of the wick is wet.

The capillary action is much less than that of the original paper wicks, and these wicks clog with minerals much faster. They do rejuvenate after soaking in white vinegar, but I will be spending as much on vinegar as I did with paper wicks.

Below is info on how to setup the device with smart life app as the instructions included are no longer correct:

Pairing to wifi is confusing due to app change.
Ways to pair w new app:

If this device shows up on wifi search (SMART-XXXX) use this otherwise scroll down:

Add device>>Small appliance>>humidifier(wifi only)>>add wifi credentials>>(ignore reset/power and hit next)>>slowblink status button(one on the left)>>goto connect button at bottom of page

At this point it will tell you to connect to the unit via wifi and will pull up your wifi manager. Select the wifi device from the list (think it was "smart" something or another. Easy enough to find as it'll be a random new device you've never seen before) after you connect hit your "back" button and you should drop back to the app and it'll finish the connection and add the device.

If it Does not show up on a wifi scan:

1. Power off the machine via the power button but keep plugged in.

2. Press and hold the "Mist level" button for about 5 seconds, the WiFi signal icon will start flashing.

3. Enter the App, enter the "+" on the upper right corner of the home page or select the add a device
button if you have no current devices.

4. Choose "Small Home Appliance" on the left side of the screen.

5. Scroll down a bit on the main screen are and select "Humidifier(Wi-Fi)".

6. Enter the Wi-Fi Name and Password if needed and click next

7. Reset device( Double check the wifi icon is blinking still. If not repeat steps 1 and 2 so it is)

8. Select "Blink Quickly" button on bottom right

9. Wait for the app to find and configure machine (can take up to 2 minutes).

Hopefully this helps some people out!

I never even knew what the heck this was, or heard of it, until I got so far with trying to figure out why in the world my washing machine wasn't spinning properly, even though it agitated and filled and switched cycles. I thought I may have had to replace the drive block in which case, I needed to remove the agitator completely and then tada the spanner nut. Please pick this up, don't try and use channel locks or something, your going to end up chipping your porcelain drum, and then hello rust city, thus ruining your washing machine entirely. This is exactly what it looks like solid steel, and it even comes with an adapter piece for smaller nuts. I just dropped it on the nut in the center of the washer, make sure you have a Rubber Mallet on hand, some people use a regular hammer, you can, but you have to be extremely cautious you don't slip and again chip your drum, so for me, better safe then sorry, I used a rubber mallet, and it worked just fine. Hold on to the wrench so that your applying pressure not only to the opposite handle your hitting, but so that the pressure exerts into the center so it doesn't slip, if you don't do this, it will slip. Just give it a few good whacks to break it free and then you can just spin the wrench once you've loosened it up. It works great. Btw, if you find yourself in my shoes with a washing machine that won't spin, is making a odd noise when turned on, but does all of the other operations it should, I'm sorry to say, it's not the drive block, but the transmission. I actually had a friend that repairs them for a living come and verify that it was my transmission. That being said, if you hop on your local for sale ad's check for a washer made by the same company yours is. I own a whirlpool and got really lucky, found an estate washer for sale for $25, both made by whirlpool, and was able to not only replace my transmission and have a great running washer, but I was also to take tons of other parts for possible future repairs, especially the motor, so if you do that, take everything from the underside of your washer, even the hot water/cold water black switches up top, cause I actually had that go to on mine. You can easily Google search to find what other sub brands your company makes so you know your getting the right parts. I've fixed my washer quite a few times for various things from the clutch, to agitator dogs, the switch, to the transmission. If you need help write a comment and I'd be happy to help you, walk you thru the steps. And a very simple fix/diagnosis, if your washer turns on, fills up but then won't switch into the next cycle or switch into any cycles, don't do what the people who sold me that awesome washer for $25 did and sell it for $25, your washing machine is 100% working great, you have no major problem at all, it's 99.9% probably the lid switch, the switch that click when you close your lid, so when you lift the lid it stops, and it takes no more than 15 minutes to fix, and even less time if you do what I did and just remove the stupid thing, so now my washer doesn't stop when I lift the lid, and that problem will never be a problem for me again. So I literally got a washer that was fully functional, all the parts worked perfect, it was just that stupid wire switch that was the problem, and there washer was in brand new condition! Hope I was able to help you with how to use this, and maybe some insight on any issues you may be having. I do reviews for Amazon so I am on here checking comments daily, and I also have my email address on my profile if you need immediate help and I haven't checked comments yet that day, please feel free to email me!

I really hate writing this review because the machine is awesome. It’s small, fits nicely on the counter, quiet, and makes perfect ice. Until it doesn’t. After only six weeks of use, it stopped making ice. We contacted Opal customer service via email and waited a week for a reply. After several emails back and forth to diagnose the problem, we were told the machine needed to be defrosted and given steps to follow. We ran through the defrost and cleaning cycle 8-10 times. No luck, so we contacted customer service again and were told to run a vinegar wash through the machine. Vinegar wash three times, still nothing. Back to customer service via email (the only way to contact the company) to be instructed to clean the sensors. Sensors cleaned, still no ice. At this point it’s been two weeks since we first contacted customer service and we’re a bit frustrated but we reach out again. “You’ll need to purchase the Opal Cleaning Kit, sold on our website.” NO! Replace the darn thing! At nearly $500 it should work longer than 6 weeks and we shouldn’t have to jump through hoops when it breaks. Don’t buy it! You will be held hostage by your love of nugget ice!

I'm a Turkish/Cowboy coffee guy.
For those that don't know what that means, I mix my finely ground coffee directly into hot water, mix and let the grounds sink to the bottom. The Crema is superb. It is seriously the best and most flavorful way to drink a cup of coffee...... That is in My Humble Opinion.....

Apparently most people don't like having to worry about the last couple sips containing grounds.
The next best thing is a french press or a pour over, but most pour overs use a paper filter which keeps a great deal of the crema from getting into the cup. Not this one, This pour over by HRySer uses a fine Stainless stell screen inside a stainless steel strainer cone.

I tested it with my normal super fine ground coffee and it worked perfectly. The coffee was rich and flavorful.

I love the way it is designed.
I love the way it works.
I love the fact that it doesn't use paper filters.
I love the fact that I finally have a way to make my girlfriends coffee in the morning!

Conclusion:
A great pour over coffee maker that would work in any kitchen (or for camping too)

The replacement for the part w11025984 Rev B tends to be over $100, but having replaced many of these, I know that most of these are the exact same part just with a different shaped plug. You can see in my pictures the old one on the left (with the part number sticker) and the new one I ordered on the right. Everything about the wiring harness (including the sensor) can be swapped, so that's exactly what I did. So instead of paying over $100 for the more expensive part, I bought this one here for $30 or so and then swapped out the wiring harnesses (just plug and play, really) and that was that.

Spoiler alert, this is what a lot of appliance repair technicians do as well. Swapping out and using the old harness took me 7 or 8 minutes, most of that was for taking the pictures. I'm not trying to say this his how companies like to rip people off, but most of the internal parts in your $2,000 washing machine and dryer set are usually the exact same as the parts in your $250 set. Before you spend the big money on repairs, take a look at what you have, and then find out what all matched your part numbers, then order the corresponding lower-priced part because (as you can see here) it's the EXACT same thing. The only thing you might have to do is change the wiring harness. 7 or 8 minutes of work sure beats spending an extra $60 or $70 for the same part that just has a different shaped plug.

Please don’t buy this washer or dryer from GE. You will need service and the process will be very painful.

I purchased this washer and dryer set with pedestals in January 2021 to be installed in my new house. About a month after installation the washer wouldn’t turn on. I scheduled service with GE and a week later they sent out a local 3rd party technician. They diagnosed the issue as the main board and ordered the part. Another week later the replacement part arrived. I informed the tech and after three weeks he came back and installed the board. The washer would finally turn on but after a week it started to only go through the first few steps in its start up cycle but would never actually start the wash cycle. We called again for service and over the next 4 months and several service calls, the technicians replaced several parts, including the door assembly but the problem still persisted. During this time we had to swap between using the washer when it would work or going to the laundromat when it wouldn't.

Finally in June, after 5 months of living with the constant issues, GE agreed to replace the washer. The washer arrived and a local installer came to install it. When they saw that the old washer was on a pedestal he informed me that they could not put a new washer on an old pedestal. The GE customer relations person had forgotten to ask if the washer was on a pedestal so a replacement one was never shipped. The installer said they would take the washer back and hold it in their warehouse until GE ordered the pedestal. I called the customer relations person back again (each call takes over an hour on hold to get through). They informed me that they would order the pedestal and email me with an ETA. Ater another week, which put us in July, GE customer relations emailed to tell me it would be the end of July before the pedestal would arrive. They offered to refund the pedestal. I explained that without the pedestal the washer would sit almost two feet lower that the dryer right next to it which was not an option for me.

After several email exchanges I was informed that the pedestal was back ordered and would not arrive until mid August. I called customer relations again on a Friday afternoon to discuss a plan. I asked if I could simply go buy the pedestal (new in a box and GE branded). They say that would work and told me that they would schedule the washer again to be delivered. I went and bought the pedestal that same day. On the following Monday I was informed by GE customer relations that they had spoken to the installer and the installer said the pedestal I purchased would not work and gave no other information about how this would be resolved. I”m on hold again with GE as we speak to try again to get some kind of resolution. My next call will be the Better Business Bureau and Consumer Affairs in my state. Bottom line, do not buy a GE appliance.

After the light switch on our KitchenAid double door, bottom freezer, just disintegrated, I searched all over the web for the right replacement part.  I found the correct part number on the Kitchenaid-Whirlpool web site, as well as the prices. ($30 and above, plus $5-$7 for shipping)  Then I searched several 3rd party web sites.  Then I searched Amazon.  The Amazon price was considerably lower than all the others ($22 and no shipping fee).  While searching on the web, I found several "how-to-install" videos.  But, BE PREPARED.  This simple little part does not come out easily, and as one commenter said, do NOT remove the old part until the new one arrives.  You'll see where the little spring bar is located (on the new part) on the bottom of the switch.  That's the bar you must push out of the way before the thing will come out of the refrigerator wall.  Even then, it was a struggle.  Protect all four sides of the hole with duct tape because if you don't, you'll chew up your refrigerator wall trying to dig out the broken part.  The part has three electrical connector prongs sticking out the back.  But once you get it removed, you'll see that there is a black connector that is "L" shaped, and it curves upward from the prongs, making it extremely difficult to get the whole mechanism out, then back in again.  The switch WILL -- repeat WILL -- fit back in the hole.  DO NOT trim the hole size to make the new part fit back in.  It will fit, but it's VERY tight.  I had to use a flexible blade putty knife, a stiff blade putty knife, needle-nosed pliers, and a very large flat-bladed screwdriver, all at once, and thank goodness that my helper could hold one of the tools plus the flashlight.  (The power is unplugged throughout the whole process, of course.)  So, I spent $22 for a part from Amazon, rather than $145,  parts and labor, from my local friendly appliance repair company.  You can do it.  Just be prepared for some labor.

Love it, with one codicil: the grates could be a bit deeper into the center of each burner. That said, pots are stable, and for very small things, such as an espresso pot, there is a ring provided that sits on top of the grates.

The big burner brings a teapot full of cold water to a boil in under 5 minutes, as well as nicely searing a steak or pork chops in a frying pan. It has a low enough low setting to allow a stock pot filled with soup to simmer reliably without boiling over, as well. The look is simple and sleek, and the controls include a safety feature that most US made cooktops lack. In order for the stove to light and stay lit, you must depress the control AND keep it depressed for 5 to 10 seconds, something that little hands are not capable of.

Best of all, the location of the big burner being to the side, rather than in the center, as in nearly every five burner cooktop, allows for cooking other things on the opposite side, whereas that center burner, with a large pot on it, would preclude that.

FTR: did not buy from AZ. Bought elsewhere for significantly less.

It has been a pretty good fridge so far. We have, however, had 3 issues in 6 months so they definitely have production quality problems. The water line for the dispenser was not connected right so it leaked all over our floor. Then, it got increasingly & painfully loud - you could hear it 2 rooms over - and the door handle kept coming off so we had a repairman come. One of the fans was malfunctioning - apparently not one he saw very often - and he put on a new handle. Other than that, the fridge has been good for us. The only other annoying thing is if something leaks on the expandable/collapsible shelf, it is not spill proof. This created a huge mess in our fridge. After a lot of research into 18" dishwasher options, we went with the Danby Designer 18". It got better reviews than most of the name brands we were familiar with and it was a heck of a lot cheaper too!

Our install was a breeze (of course, we had a plumber do it - he commented on what a nice unit it was too, with the built in water softener option), and dishes come out clean and spotless!

Others have commented on the long cycle time - yes, "normal" cycle is over two hours long, but we haven't had to use that cycle in order to get dishes clean - the "rapid" cycle of 35 minutes seems to get everything clean for us, glasses, bowls, etc. I'm sure when we have more heavy duty cleanup required, we'll use some of the other cycles, but either way, it works great.

If you are short on space, I highly recommend this unit!

The element in my stove had gone out recently. We cook lots of over meals so immediately I searched for a logo on the stove, and looked it in hopes of finding a replacement - excepting to pay a pretty penny to boot.

A week later, the element arrived on my door. Pulled out the stove, got everything unplugged and ready to go. Replacing the element wasn't too difficult. It was secured by two screws, then two wire-clips on the inside retaining bracket.. Swapped the units and got everything back in order - turned on the stove, and it fired up. Haven't run into any issues or noticed anything wrong or different than the original - except that my stove is acting like its brand new again!

Don't be hesitant, just match up the size and dimensions and go for it.

To start I received the ice machine and had it installed as part of an outdoor kitchen by a plumber.  It worked great first few weeks.  Came home one night to water pouring out of the back.  Opened it up and found the internal water line burst.  I easily replaced it with 1\4 OD and restarted.  It would not run.  I emailed customer support and they responded immediately.  He walked me through doing a reset on the board which didnt work.  Then said he would send a brand new board.  The board arrived shortly after and I changed it out.  He checked back with me and I informed him it was working but now was simply not shutting off.  I do want to add even when sending me the board they stated they would give me half my purchase price back for my troubles.  At this point he informed me they would send me a brand new unit which they now said has a better water line.  A week later it arrived as promised and yes the water line was of better quality and integrity.  I know thier is some bad in here for the machine failing.  I think yes the original line bursting just caused a succession of issues.  What I do want to attest to is this seller STANDS by thier product with responsive support and resolution!  Could not have been more pleased with thier timely responses and troubleshooting.  Eventually they simply replaced it completely.  No argument and no questions asked.  That is A+ in my book.  Sometimes things just go wrong.  When someone actually supports what they sold, helps you troubleshoot and in the end makes it right that is a product you want to buy.  So far replacement machine is working great and is everything I expected it to be.  I rarely ever write a review but in this case I felt I needed to convey that this seller went above and beyond to make sure I was happy with thier product and solving the issue that happened.  Dave M.

Our 15 year old LG DLG5966W gas dryer had been making squeaking noises on start up, sometimes continuing to squeak while running so it was time to either call the repair man or give it go myself. A quick search and I found this kit that had everything needed, along with a great YouTube video by PartSelect with a guy named Steve that walked through most of what I needed to do. Tools I used for this job were:

1. A decent cordless drill https://amzn.to/3ZdVBvn
2. A set of metric box end ratcheting wrenches https://amzn.to/3IMt8r8
3. A magnetic mat to hold/sort screws as removed https://amzn.to/3IN37b2
4. A vacuum or hand vac https://amzn.to/3lWjHfN

Once I got the dryer apart (labeling and placing on the magnetic mat as I go) and lifted out the drum, I could see that the right rear roller wheel was not turning at all! It had a flat spot on it from the drum rubbing against it. What Steve didn't go into in the video I mention is how to remove and replace the complete roller wheel assemblies, which requires an extra couple steps and is why you'll need the wrenches I mentioned above. The front roller wheels are easy to access and replace, however the back roller wheels will need you to remove the inside back panel by taking out (6?) screws holding it in place from the back of the dryer. Once those screws were out, I just needed to gently tilt forward and lift out the shroud, being careful to decouple from the heat pipe, in order to remove and replace the back roller wheel assemblies with a couple of the wrenches.

While you're in there, vacuum out the lint - I had to empty my hand vac a couple times due to how much there was. I also removed a bunch of hair that had wrapped around the motor shaft.

The kit came with a new belt, belt tensioner, and spring. My belt was frayed in a couple places, so really glad the kit had a new one in it.

I cleaned all the dust and lint off of the drum, and put it all back together. I am happy to report, the dryer is running like new again! It's so quiet that we have to go in the laundry room to see if it's still running.

Hoping these parts last another 15 years. 90 minutes and about $30 - it was well worth it! Anybody with even a little mechanical ability can do this job. Go for it!

Update after 1 month:

Belt started slipping after a week or so, could have been installer error as the belt was out of the motor pulley and was just on the smooth shaft. Belt was frayed and grooves were smooth on much of it. I'll take the blame on that one, but put on an "OEM" replacement belt, ensuring to have it properly seated in the pulley before closing things up. I also notices a little wear on the front wheels, some fragments coming off the sides. Time will tell if that will turn in to a long term problem.

Another tip - wear gloves when disassembling - both times in taking off the bulkhead, I cut my hand on a sharp edge. Not a problem assembling, just in trying to wrestle the bulkhead out of its retaining clips and lift it out is when I got nicked.

We purchased this Induction cooktop after much research. It had all the features we were looking for in an induction cooktop at a reasonable price. We checked appliance stores in the area and the soonest they were able to get one was 8 weeks. By ordering it through Amazon we could get it in 4 days - however, we knew then that if there was a problem, we'd have to go through the Frigidaire company as we had no appliance store's service to back it up. The item came within the time stated and was in great shape. We installed it to replace our electric cooktop. After a couple days we realized the largest burner was only creating heat in the front of the element - not the back. We contacted Frigidaire's service and got through easily - no long holds!! They were very responsive and made an appointment while I was on the phone for a local service company to come and look at it. I was amazed and grateful that this went so well! Within the original date for service we received a call from the service company for more information, and the service was done within the anticipated time, under the warranty. The adjustment took 30 minutes. The cooktop works perfectly. We are extremely satisfied with the experience and we couldn't have expected a better outcome! The cooktop is beautiful, amazingly fast and functions perfectly - as expected!! No more 'over-boils', scalding, or burning as the induction elements respond instantaneously as you adjust the temps. Far, far quicker response than gas or conventional electric. We couldn't be more pleased with our purchase!!

Several years ago we planned that one day we'd replace our conventional cooktop with Induction, therefore, as we updated our pans, we purchased only induction-capable, making the transition seamless.

I read many of the reviews for this dishwasher as well as another brand and there were many negative and positive reviews. I thought about it for a few days and decided to risk purchasing this brand. I was worried about it turning into a big hunk of junk but I HATE doing dishes by hand so it was worth the risk to me. My boyfriend and I live in a VERY small apartment so this machine is the perfect size. It's about three times the size of a microwave so it does hog counter space. I have mine in a spot that doesn't have an upper cabinet so I'm able to put things on top of the dishwasher like my mixer and blender which is a plus.

So, my boyfriend was able to carry the heavy box by himself up the flight of stairs to our apartment. It was well packaged in an ordinary brown box and then inside that box was styrofoam sheets for padding around the dishwasher box. It was going well until my eyes set on a HUGE DENT in the side of the machine!! I was soooo mad especially since both boxes were not damaged at all! HOW DID THIS HAPPEN!!? I should have gave it four stars just for that alone. I have read this in other reviews so I was worried that mine would be dented as well and it was. It's not worth sending back since a replacement will probably be dented as well. I got lucky though because the dent is on the left side toward the back so it's not too noticeable unless you're standing at my sink. I'm still irritated by it though since this machine was so expensive.

Anyway, my boyfriend set it up quickly and used plumbers tape at all the connections. So far we've ran it three times with no leaks. The machine is NOT noisy as some others have stated. The only loud part is when the water comes rushing out from the hookup at the faucet and that's to be expected. I find the lulling of the machine to be no different than a regular dishwasher. You do have to rinse the dishes very well before you load them since it doesn't remove any stuck on foods. I didn't do this the first two times I ran it and had to hand wash all those dishes. I used a whole dishwasher detergent tablet (by Finish...but I'll be changing to something more eco-friendly) and some rinse aid. We have really bad well water so the detergent worked really well with the rinse aid. No residue or any spots and everything came out really clean. I didn't have any trouble with loading the dishes and some people stated that their large plates wouldn't fit. My large plates fit but it is a tight fit. The plates are just barely touching the top of the machine and you don't have to lean them over. Leaning them over actually won't allow them to be cleaned properly. It does hold quite a bit and I'm able to run it every few other day or so. The silverware caddy is removable and it has two sides and each side is devided so there are four spots to put silverware and etc. Two of the sections are raised slightly higher than the other two which is great for smaller/shorter items. The caddy also had a snap on cover on each of its sides which I removed since I don't have anything tiny to be washed like bottle nipples and etc. The machine is sturdy and does not wobble. The door takes a hard tug to get it open and the buttons are easy to use and understand. The run time is pretty long at an hour and a half but I don't mind. So far it's a great little machine and I'll update this review that I continue to use it.

Take out the 3 screws that hold the gas valve to the frame, remove one screw at the back that holds the gas line to the back frame, slid the gas valve unit forward enough to work on it. I don't know why one would spend big dollars having a "pro" do this, only slightly more complicated than changing a light bulb.
To get the front off, remove 2 screws at back of top, slide top towards the back back, the control panel hinges forward and detaches and can be set on top with wires attached, several screws are revealed at top of front panel, remove them and a couple at the bottom of door opening, hinges front panel forward to reveal door light wiring, unplug, remove front panel, proceed as above.

So, be warned, this thing may help or may not. It may be easy for you to install, but it sure wasn't for me.

This works great on a Samsung Front Load Dryer, I used it to repair a burnt wire that's connected to the Heating Element. It has aluminum wire (so does the wire in the dryer), so you can connect them together with the twist connector that's included. I bought an extra one to keep on hand in case I need it again.
In Sept 2016 I replaced the Heating Element and in July 2019 one of the wires connected to the heating element was burnt and completely disconnected from the terminal. I was able to find a wire connector kit at an appliance part's store, but it had copper wire so I had to hunt down a copper to aluminum connector (purple box in picture). In Jan 2020 I found the other wire was burnt, so finding this part on amazon was great as the wire is aluminum and I won't need that special connector (which is pricey).
I researched possible root causes for this problem and found I may have caused it when replacing the Heating Element. If the terminals are loose when you attached them to the Heating Element, then excess heat can cause the wires to overload and burn. So this time I snugged them down with a plyers, just enough so they weren't loose. Hopefully I won't need to repair this again.

Now the detailed dimension I wanted to know before I bought it but I couldn't find.
Height: 75mm

Height to the underside of the top rim(this decides if the basket sits on the bottom of the cup or the top of the cup): 73mm

Foot print: 47mm in diameter, quickly tapered(about 45 degree angle) to 52mm(bottom of the mesh cylinder).

Top of the mesh cylinder: 57mm in diameter

Above the mesh cylinder but underside of the rim(this decides the minimum diameter of the cup when the basket sits on top of the cup): 62mm in diameter.
This means, if you have a cup little smaller than 62mm inside diameter at the top, the basket may not go down all the way but maybe partly goes in the cup. Under 57mm, you probably won't get a good result.

Far end of the handle to the opposite: 98mm
Length of each handle: 18mm
This means, if you have a cup between 62mm and 80mm in diameter at the top, the basket sits on the top without positioning. Between 80mm and 98mm, you'll need careful positioning of the basket or the basket will fall in the cup. Above 98mm, it'll always fall, unless the basket sits on the bottom of the cup.

Excellent replacement for my ~2001 era GE Gas cooktop.

Trouble shooting/installation instructions below

Problem Symptom: Unable to ignite a single burner, no clicking sound, gas works fine. You can ignite the burner with a match and/or setting an alternative burner to ignite and ignite both burners at the same time (be careful as you will be flooding gas as you reach for the second burner!)

Repair steps:
1. shut off gas
2. remove knobs (simply pull up)
3. remove grates, burners. remove 2 phillips screws per burner
4. gently pull up glass top and slide to the side (you can remove, but this will require removal of igniters as well)
5. remove wires from old gas valve switch (remember which side is which)
6. replace new gas valve switch.
7. Put everything back together

Good luck!

Dylan

I have done about ten small loads in this machine and am happy with it so far. I previously owned and reviewed the Wonder Wash manual washer and although I was very happy with it for a year, the hand cranking was getting a little difficult for me as was awkwardly having to drain into a bucket due to the side it drained on.

In fact, the Wonder Wash is faster - you only have to crank for a few minutes to get an effective level of cleaning of a much longer cycle on this machine. Wonder Wash uses less water and would appear to be gentler on clothes. It is similar to comparing a front loading machine's action (Wonder Wash) to a top loading machine (Panda Washer).

But again. Panda does the work so you don't have to.

It arrived with no damage. Having to remove the plastic protective film from the top required having to unclip the top handle to get it all out rather than leave pieces of film. Then I saw the drain hose set up that many have written about. Remembering the review where they said the fastening loop was too close to the end of the drain tube I noticed that and moved the fastening loop lower on the tube to keep slack out of it and raise its end higher so water would not slosh out during the wash. And there is the rub - this tube is a rather rigid plastic, a type of plastic I have often had crack on me on other appliances (vacuum, carpet cleaner) at the bend. I would not want to put such stress on the bend tightly all the time so during storage I used a twist tie to have the tube more relaxed but still hanging up. This is really something their design people should take into account and use a more flexible type of plastic or even rubber. Something that can be easily replaced too. I would think if I disassembled I would find it attached to some fitting in a manner that it can be removed, hopefully it is not cemented. I would not want to junk a machine of this cost for a 25 cent plastic hose cracking.

The other issue would be the odd smell. One reviewer had mentioned something smoking with heavy/frequent loads and I don't think mine is anything like that. There is a rather pronounced plasticy smell when it is working, even with cold water so it is not coming from the tub. I initially thought maybe just excess oil around the motor burning off (but it really isn't smoky). Could be motor sits on something that stinks when it gets warm. I don't know, I think it is getting a little better with time. The odor starts almost as soon as the machine does so I really doubt it has anything at all to do with overworking it. If it persists I might try contacting customer service.

So far I have washed almost everything I did in my Wonder Wash except for sheets. I could do one queen fitted or flat in that - summer weight. I would think I could do the same in this. That is really the limit for my countertop spinner too so is fine with me. I have done a pair of jeans without issue with a t-shirt or two in there too. I do 6-9 minute washes because I don't really want to beat up my clothes and they are generally not heavily soiled. I do 2-3 min rinses (two of them) and spray water in as well as it drains with my shower massager. I also fill it with the shower massage, I have not tried the little fill hose so cannot comment on it though I am eying it as an extension for my countertop spinner's drain hose.

I give the Panda Washer a thumbs-up but I have not relegated my Wonder Wash to the garage. And may very well use that for washing my long lace window panels as I think it has a superior gentler washer action. I may use it for some sweaters as well that I don't want agitated. Otherwise I will be using the Panda washer for everything but heavy blankets and flannel sheets so will be seeing very little of the dreaded laundromat!

UPDATE - my original review was rather prophetic and after 22 months of pretty heavy use I encountered leaking at bottom. Upon draining and close inspection and some testing, it appears to be a small slit at the drain hose bend at bottom. Unlike some others may I have not kept the hose pulled up tightly to get a better water level but rather started to use the useless fill hose as an extension. I don't see any way to make a sturdy repair without replacing the hose owing to where the split is located and being subjected to flexing, hot water, detergents, etc. In order to get to the connection of the hose it appears I need to tear apart the machine almost completely, the tub needs to be removed from the base and that involves pulling out/off the shaft for agitator. I decided to use some waterproof tape temporarily and have ordered a different miniwasher.

I got my money's worth out of this I believe. I just don't think the current price and shipping is worth purchasing again. If they replaced that awful hose with a rubber one or maybe even used a nylon or pvc elbow to make repair easier would be a great improvement.

Pros:

-- So quiet you don't hear it even if you're in the same room. Having a Bosch is like getting another room in your house.
-- Cleaning is amazing if you use the right detergent for your water and your water isn't too hard. Finish Tabs work extremely well for us.
-- Efficiency is phenomenal. Uses extremely little water and electricity so over the life of the dishwasher it will save you a few bucks.

Cons:

-- You have to use good quality detergent. If your detergent is poor quality I think the problems are magnified in the Bosch because it uses so little water.
-- Racks are odd and take some getting used to. They have good features but I like American style racks better.
-- There is no dry cycle. So dishes (especially plastics) can be a little wet when you take them out. That, along with 2 hour cycles is the price you pay for ultra energy efficiency (the dishwasher only uses about $20 worth of electricity in a YEAR).

Everyone should know: If you want ultra-quiet, the price you pay will be long cycles because the dishwasher is running at lower water pressure. The faster the dishwasher, the louder it is going to be. So I suppose speed versus noise is the tradeoff you should be aware of when you buy the dishwasher. If you want quiet Bosch is a great choice. Though there are other good choices as well.

These same parts were on backorder from Whirlpool for months and they could not give me a delivery date.  I ordered this repair kit and it was delivered three days later.  I then watched dozens of You Tube videos before I removed a single screw on my dryer, as you must disassemble 80% of the unit to install the tensioner pulley and belt.  The shaft of the pulley was very slightly too large and I had to sand it to get the new pulley on.  I learned that on one of the videos, as it is evidently common for the pulley bushing to be too small.  I did not use the four new drum rollers, as mine seemed to be in good condition and I didn't want to sand all of those shafts, too.  I did use a high grade bicycle grease on all of the pulleys before I bolted everything back together.  The work sounded much scarier than it really was and turned out to only take about an hour to install while working inside our tiny laundry room.  The dryer has been running like new for months now.

Ah the joys of ice! It was wonderful to have ice again. Our fridge ice maker was broken, so this seemed like a cheaper replacement than calling a refrigerator repair person. It pumped out lots and lots of ice and that was wonderful. I really loved the ice.

A couple of things that might not be clear from the product description:
1. You have to fill this manually with about three Super Big Gulps of water a couple of times a day. You can't hook it to your water line.
2. The ice chamber is not refrigerated, so on hot days the ice melts pretty fast.

There are a few cons:
1. The fan is loud. Like running the water in the kitchen sink loud. Louder than our fridge loud.
2. The bucket frequently only fills half way because stray ice triggered a false "bucket full" condition. I had to constantly clear that single piece of ice so the bucket would fill.
3. I don't know what they are talking about with "self cleaning." Ours developed that orange gunk that some humid bathrooms get on the grout between tiles and there is no automatic cleaning of that stuff.

Our machine developed a glitch after a month or two where every time you open the lid it would shut the machine off, so next time you needed ice it was empty. Also, remembering to fill it twice a day was a pain. And finally, ours died after a scant 8 months, so the price doesn't seem like such a bargain now. Who knows? You may have better luck.

First, I purchased a washer sight-unseen from a BIG Box Store because it was a brand I'd purchased before, and it was on sale.  I was SO disappointed when I used this washer that I sent it back within two days.  The BIG Box washer had a locking lid, which apparently all brands except for Speed Queen now have.  The locking lid is necessary for safety reasons because washing machine companies cheaped out and removed the brake that stops the spin cycle on the machines.  This saved them money, but makes for a crappy machine.  I absolutely HATE the locking lid. So what did I do?  I bought a Speed Queen WITHOUT a locking lid.  Now, I can open my washer and add an extra item without a problem.  I can monitor whether or not I need a stained item to soak longer because I can open the lid to look at it.  If I want to add vinegar or anything else to the rinse cycle, I can because I don't have a locking lid. I don't have to press some button and wait to open my washer. My laundry is NOT held hostage.  Yaaaa!!!!  The locking lid and removal of the brake on other washers was the dumbest idea ever.  I LOVE my Speed Queen.

I tried asking the landlord to fix it, but after weeks of nothing, I finally had enough and bought this little washer. I looked up dozens of models, read a bunch of reviews and watched videos, and finally decided on this one. I liked that the order was fulfilled by Amazon itself, so it gave me some peace of mind in case it stopped working as soon as I used it (a common problem to some people with some other models). But I have to say that I had no problems! This little machine changed my life, I honestly enjoy doing laundry now, before I was always stressed and annoyed that I paid money every week to have wet, smelly clothes. The washer part is surprisingly big and can wash thick towels and sheets (one at a time), in addition to jeans and everyday clothes. The spinner is not as big and I usually split my load into 2 or 3 parts to be spun, which is not a problem at all since it's so fast. The clothes come out almost dry and after a few hours of air drying, they're good to go (even my thick towels get dry after 3-4hrs of air drying). I do not use the hose that came with it, I use a bucket to pour water in the washer and then leave the drain hose in the bucket to drain the water of the machine. I find that easier since the hose is short and my machine is not that close to a sink. You also have to elevate the machine so the water can drain efficiently, I place mine on top of the kitchen table when I need to do laundry, which is not a problem since the machine is light. I'm very happy with this purchase and only wish I had bought it sooner. I've been using it consistently for around 3 months (multiple times a week) and so far, I didn't have any problems with it.

Our Samsung DW is 7 years old and has been giving us random error codes for the past 1-2 years. At first it appeared that it was the temperature sensor. We replaced that in Aug 2020 and everything was back to normal. Then in Aug 2021 we were getting the same error messages. Replaced the temperature sensor again and it was fine for about another month.

Then we started getting the delicate flashing error after the full wash cycle ended, indicated a drainage issue. We checked all the drainage lines, which were clear, and there was not any water left in the DW to indicate a drainage issue. I dealt with it for awhile and I'd leave the DW door open to air dry (since the dishes were clean...). I was getting real fed up with the DW and the flashing error codes!

I decided to try 1 more fix before just biting the bullet and buying a new DW. Decided it was time to try to replace the main control panel for $150 before spending $500+ on a new machine. Installation was relatively easy (didn't need to pull machine out, took about 5 minutes, easy to find YouTube video).

This fix has worked great! It's been a full month with no error messages! It's like our machine is brand new, Actually better than new since now our machine doesn't leak at the beginning of the DW cycle anymore! I have a older HotPoint side by side refrigerator that the evaporator fan was making a "whirling" noise. Sort of like the fan was spinning fast and slow. It had become quite annoying as it was louder than normal also. I searched the internet and found that it was either the fan in the back of the freezer going bad or this main control board something was bad on it. Few people online had said to take the small panel on the back of the fridge off and look at resistor on the board to see if any of them were black or appeared damaged. This would mean the fan motor was bad and a new board wouldn't fix. My fridge didn't have damaged resistors so I ordered this board.

My refrigerator is a HotPoint HSH22IFTA WW and the ground wire was a little different. I was able to unplug the ground wire from my old board and plug into the new board. I removed the inspected date sticker from the new board and taped it to the old ground wire, as that is where the date stamp is. There is nothing holding the wires on the board so with a little force I was able to pull the wire connectors off the board, install the new board and plug in the new one. I took a picture of old board so I knew where all wires would go on the new board.

I will also say this didn't completely fix my fridge problem. Due to the Evaporator fan not running correctly it caused the fins inside the freezer to get built up with ice / frost. I had to "defrost" the freezer fins by removing the back panel from the freezer and using a hair drier to help speed up the thawing process. This has been working for over a month now and the fan hasn't made any loud noises and the fridge freezer is staying cold. I'll post a picture of the old board installed that showes the ground wire that is different then the new one. The new board is sitting on the bubble wrap. The ground wire is just held on the board by spade terminals and you can see the old wire beside the new board. I hope this helps you out in replacing your control board. I prefer to eat a larger meal at lunchtime and something smaller at dinner, so I am always looking for creative ways to make more substantial meals. And I’m tired of doing takeout or all of the premade meals at the grocery store. So I decided to give this a shot.

The label on the dish says that it will cook your ear of corn and potatoes in just three minutes. I found that hard to believe, but decided to try. I put an ear of corn on one side and two small-to-medium red potatoes on the other side. I only knew that add a tablespoon of water to each side because of the Amazon description. It didn’t say that anywhere on the package when it arrived.

After three minutes, the ear of corn was steamed quite well. I may have even liked it more had I left it in for another 30 seconds or so, but it was still really good. The potatoes were still hard, though, after only three minutes. So that label on the package is a bit misleading, though the Amazon description does say up to five minutes.

I heated the potatoes for another three minutes, and they came out super soft, ready to be mashed if I wanted. I am going to have to keep this in mind for making fresh mashed potatoes at work, if I am so inclined.

So overall, it did work. You just have to figure out what size of potatoes will work best and for how long. I am looking forward to testing it with some other fresh veggies.

For the price this is a surprisingly capable little machine. I found it to be quieter than expected, just a low-frequency hum that I can barely hear. As the description states, this is a machine of the bullet type, so you get round ice pieces with a hole halfway through since the pieces form around the individual refrigerant pipe ends, but unless you specifically want cubes that is no issue. If anything the bullets have a larger surface area and will cool your drinks faster if that is a priority.

Don't expect this to keep up with ice for a party, but for the occasional cold drink on hot summer days this will do nicely. From power-on to first ice will take around 8-10 minutes depending on your ambient temperature, much faster than an ice tray in the freezer.

I’ve always bought the official replacement water filters for my refrigerator, but it seemed like they were getting ridiculously expensive. We live in an area with very hard tap water, so going without a filter isn’t a great idea. These filters seemed reasonably-priced, so I wanted to give them a shot.

I wanted to see how they compare to the OEM filters, so I did a little comparison. I collected a sample using the OEM filter and then put one of these replacement filters. In order to prime the system, you have to run a few gallons through to get it flushed out. After everything reached equilibrium, I took a sample from the replacement filter. No noticeable difference in water clarity from either sample, both were clear of debris. I’ll admit I don’t have the most refined palette, but I didn’t notice a distinguishable difference in taste between the two samples, either.

These filters seem like they are priced right and the 3-pack will last me quite a while. When I eventually run out, I’ll definitely look into buying more.

[[VIDEOID:f1b26d3e781e35804a13e533118211c5]] This will be a two-part review, just received this item and I am really looking forward to getting it setup for use. So for the first half of my review, I will talk about this unit before I use it. I was looking for an ice machine that would supplement my refrigerator one. With company my refrigerator won't keep up in the ice department so I picked this unit out. Some features I really liked are that it is pretty compact, makes and holds a decent amount of Ice and does not need to be refilled with water. I checked the electrical rating and it draws 1.2 amps, that is really low so it can be plugged into any outlet without the need for a separate circuit. It does need a water line but it is not too hard to add one, I really did not want to have to monitor refilling. I hope to install this into a cabinet for a nice tucked in look, the handle can be mounted either side so I don't need to worry too much where it is installed based on door swing. For now I don't have the cabinet so it will be freestanding, I checked and the feet are adjustable to prevent any wobble on uneven surfaces. Lastly it arrived undamaged, seemed to be packed pretty good to prevent it from getting beat up. Mine arrived upside down, due to having a compressor I would always recommend letting the unit sit right side up for a day or two before turning on. Once I get it hooked up I will review the second part of operation and performance.

8/15/2022 - update/ so for part two, had this unit for a little over a week now. I had a little issue with the water supply line, this was on my end and had nothing to do with the actual unit itself. As I had mentioned previously I would need to add a water line for this machine, so I tried the clamp on style connector which lasted about a week and then it decided it was just going to leak all the time. I replaced with and would recommend grabbing the SharkBite style which also has a shut off, it is a little more effort but way more worry free. So back to the ice machine, it is really quiet, I could barely hear the unit over my dehumidifier. This would be perfect under a cabinet in the kitchen or bar as you would not hear the unit running. It did as stated make a bucket of ice in 24hrs, I would say it took about 30 hrs for the ice to build up high enough to stop the production of ice. Overall this is a really nice unit and makes a decent amount of Ice to support heavy usage.

Like everyone else has said, the filter works fine, installs fine, water tastes fine, all fine.

But you're paying $50 for a $15 filter. Period. It is exactly the same filter as the RPWF. Not similar, not newer - they are EXACTLY THE SAME FILTER. After the filter is manufactured, a few get pulled from the line and a little RFID chip is taped to the side. That's the difference. Oh, and fridge itself has added software that (1) turns off the water if the RFID is not detected and (2) logs the RFID serial number to ensure the same RFID is not used twice. This absolutely ensures you must use GE's filter if you want filtered water. No more installing a competitor's filter. No installing the same GE filter without the RF chip. No more removing the RFID and reusing it to trick the fridge. Nope. None of that.

So you have no alternative. It is a monopoly. GE decides to charge $100/filter next month? You have no choice. $500/filter next year? No choice. If you want filtered water, you pay their price.

Do you REALLY need to change the filter every 6 months? Of course not. GE will set an expiration far in advance of the actual filter effectivity degrading, for two reasons: (1) they want to ensure plenty of buffer, so that you never accuse their $50 filters of not lasting, and (2) they have an obvious financial incentive to sell you as many as possible as often as possible. So yes, it will last 8, maybe 9 months. But if the water starts tasting foul, time to pay their price.

I will begin by saying this is a beautiful range but has a very loud fan that you cannot turn off and gas burners that are not responsive. I have had terrible customer service with Frigidaire and Pacific Sales was unwilling to help us when Frigidaire would not. We only had the range for 11 months, still under manufacture warranty, and sold it to buy a Samsung.

The issues we had began back when we received the range in October 2014 from Pacific Sales (Best Buy). We were unable to use it the first four weeks as we were having construction done in out kitchen. Once we finally connected it, we noticed that the cooling fan was very loud. I called Frigidaire and they informed me this was normal but after much discussion agreed to send a tech to look at it.

The Tech agreed it was a bit louder than usual and was able to make adjustments to it which made a slight improvement. But after cooking a standard meal at 400 degrees, the fan would continue to run for 45 minutes after we turned off the oven. So every time we used the oven, we would eat dinner, clean up, and sit down to watch TV, and the fan would still be running. And this is not a subtle fan, you must speak loudly to talk over it. And three days later the fan became very loud again. I called Frigidaire back and they agreed to send someone to look at it again. He came out said he needed to order a new fan and would have to set up another appointment. This was very difficult as my wife was very pregnant and we both had very busy schedules. He came back a couple weeks later and changed out the fan. It again sounded a little better but still much too loud and ran much too long after using the range. This was very annoying, but we could not return it as we were past the two week period.

A few weeks later we noticed that one of our burners wasn't very responsive when we adjusted the dial. I again reached out to Frigidaire and they agreed to send someone to look at it. I asked if the tech could please bring all of the necessary parts to fix it on the first visit as we now had a small infant and was difficult to plan our schedule. The tech came and again explained that he would need to order a part and we would need to set up another appointment. He returned a couple weeks later with the part and slightly improved the burner.

We then decided to check all the burners and noticed that none of them are responsive. For instance, as you turn the dial there seems to be four different settings; high, medium, low, and very low... but there is no proportional control.

I again reached out to Frigidaire and they again sent someone to look at our stove. This time when he looked at it he said that this is typical of a Frigidaire range and there was nothing he could do. I feel this is unacceptable as the entire purpose of us changing to a gas range was to have better proportional control than an electric range. Now we had very little control over the temperature while we are cooking at all.

I called Frigidaire back at that time to explain that we wanted a different range and they told me there was nothing they can do unless the tech considered it unrepairable. In my eyes it was unrepairable because there's no proportional control over the gas heating element and is also no control over the loud fan that runs for 45 minutes after we are done cooking. I was informed to leave the oven door open after we finished cooking to reduce the time of the fan running. That did not sound like a smart solution with an infant learning to crawl, not to mention the excessive heat. Frigidaire did not agree and once again I moved on as they said there was nothing they were willing to do.

Then, about five months later we were cooking and we had F30 error appear and the oven would not work. I was extremely upset and immediately called Frigidaire again to have it repaired. They explained that they were completely booked for two weeks and they would see if they could expedite it and would have someone call me back within 48 hours with a sooner time. After I got off the phone, I realized that the fan was still blowing on the oven and would not turn off. I eventually unplugged the range and plugged it back in and the fan turned on off.
I didn't hear back at 48 hours and after 72 hours I called back they said they had scheduled an appointment for us on September 2. We had two problems with this. First of all, that was they original date (two weeks out) that they told me when I originally called and secondly we had were not available that day. I explained that was not acceptable so they were able to get us an appointment on August 31st.

A few days before they were scheduled to come out, my wife was able to turn on the stove and the error was gone. I canceled the appointment but I know that there was some type of problem in our range computer. We then noticed that the drawer on the range was not sitting straight and it appears to have a missing screw which was one more thing that irritated us.
That is when I decided to go to back to Best Buy to see if there was anything they could do to help us out as Frigidaire was very clear that there was nothing they were willing to do.

We had only had this range for 11 months at this point and have already had techs come to our house eight times in an attempt to fix it. I had spent ours on the phone and had to open our schedules to work around techs to fix a brand new range that we spent $1,600 on. It is not as if we purchased the least expensive range on the market. We had problems with the fan, burners, computer, and the drawer, and are completely unsatisfied with this purchase. We had no confidence that this is going to last us for reasonable period of time. We wanted a refund.

I went to back to Best Buy and I spoke to the Pacific Sales manager and explained the many problems I've had with the Frigidaire range we purchased 11 months ago. I informed him we wanted a refund so we could purchase a different range. He said he would reach out to Frigidaire and see what he could do. A customer service rep from Frigidaire emailed me and asked what they could help me with. I wrote a very long email (similar to this review) and explained everything that had happened. They suggested that I buy the extended warranty and were unwilling to do anything else.

I reached back out to the Pacific Sales Manager and he said he could not do anything about our Frigidaire range, but would give us a great deal on a new range. But it turned out that his great deal was still more expensive than the same range at Home depot or Lowes. I asked if could could at least get if for half price, but he said the best he could do was match the price at Home Depot. We bought a Samsung range at Lowes the next day and it is fantastic. I will never shop at Pacific Sales or purchase a Frigidaire product again and I suggest you stay away as well.

So these bearings are a very tight fit for the flange for my washer (Samsung wf330anw). So much so I blamed the seller of the flange for selling a defective part, because these bearings fit the original part. The trouble is I was replacing the flange and the larger bearing would not slide on even after lubrication on the new flange. I bought some SRS bearings from yellowhandyman as I did previously on a hunch that maybe the bearings are the problem. Sure enough the SRS bearings slide onto the flange for the old part as well as the new part. Long story short the tolerance on the larger bearing is way too high for Xike. The smaller bearing slides on like a dream. These parts need to slide together during assembly, there is no banging or pressing these parts together.
I have taken my machine apart and rebuilt it, so I do have some experience in this matter. If you plan to rebuild be sure to check the fitment of the flange and bearings before committing to a rebuild otherwise you may find yourself in a pickle.

I like this egg holder for the refrigerator. It protects the eggs and makes it easy to get to them. It has two rubber corners that stick on the back corners to lift it slightly. It also includes a lid so you can protect the eggs, but also store items on top to save space. However, I wouldn't put anything heavy on it; nothing more than half a pound. Overall it's a sturdy egg holder. Easy to clean.

This is a two layer egg container. Each tray holds 18 large chicken eggs. We had a plastic box from an old refrigerator before and it held a lot of eggs, but you couldn't see how many were in there and you had to feel around to see if there were any in there or take the whole thing out to see inside it. It was not a great way to keep eggs. This one is clear and allows you to see how much you have. That is a big plus.

The only reason I gave this an overall four stars instead of five is because it does take up shelf space in the refrigerator. As I said above, this can be mitigated by using the top of it to store light items. I'm wondering if instead of designing it to hold three eggs across, it was two eggs across and four stacks instead of two. Perhaps if they had both designs, it could accommodate different ways people utilize their refrigerator shelving. Just a thought and not a big negative.

Overall, this egg holder is great. The clear, easy to clean plastic holds the eggs safely without breakage. It allows you to see how many you have. It holds a lot of eggs (which is great if you are cooking and baking a lot of food). The protective cover on top allows you to store other items on top of it. We've been looking for ways to organize our refrigerator and also easily keep tabs on what we have and what we need. This product does both. It's an excellent purchase and worth the money.

Originally I was satisfied with this water filter, but I was just looking for a less expensive option that fit our model. I figured that with all of the positive reviews, and the claim of NSF certification, that this would be fine for what I needed. I was in for a huge surprise shortly after installing and not giving a second thought to this filter - that fit perfectly. I was reading an article about local water contaminants and their long term effects and decided that I should probably spring for an inexpensive water testing kit, just to make sure I'm not exposing my family to unnecessary toxins. I purchased one of the little water sticks and ran a test on our tap water - it registered just in the acceptable range. Just. As in 274 ppm. Who knows what those 274 parts per molecule are, but I was not pleased. At least we only drink the water from the filter... At this point I was leary about the water testing stick, so I did some research and purchased another. I used both to test the tap and filtered water and the results were infuriating. Our tap water still registered high with the two meters reading 276 and 265, both far too high in my opinion. The filtered water registered 245 and 237. It barely made a difference! Now, I feel like I spent far too much on a product that does little more than fit in the right spot. I was so irritated, I could not understand how they could possibly be NSF certified, so I decided to research that as well. NSF certifications for residential, point of use water filters are designated by rating, the lowest being for aesthetics - smell and clarity, which if certified, would have an NSF/ANSI 42. This claims to be "NSF certified" with no designation. At this point, I didn't expect to find this filter, but in the interest of due diligence, I searched NSF.org for the manufacturer Purity Pro, the part number PF02, the OEM replacement part, all with zero results. Nothing. Needless to say, I won't be making this mistake again.

I have used twin tub washers a while ago, but they were made of metal. It was novel to see the entire case and tubs made of plastics. The agitator is vigorous, reverses spin every few seconds. The spinner is very good at expelling water, and fast, about 2 minutes average. The attachment hose for filling is not really useful, it pops off the spigot. So I use a washpan to put water in the washing tub. Less frustrating. I don't fill up the tub, either, according to the items. Keeping in mind, that the weight tolerance of the tubs MUST include the weight of the water, which is about US 2 pounds per quart. More sloshing =more cleaning action. It doesnt take very hot water, so some things I presoak to kill germs.  I found the spinner load is helped by pre-wringing or dripping out water first. If the spinner knocks around, the load is too heavy or unbalanced. I would never put a blanket in that, unless it meant a light fleece throw. 1 pair of dripped out jeans will come out very dry in 3minutes.  I use the washer side and the spinner side as I go. Yeah, it takes more handling of each garment. Not like stuffing a machine with unsorted clothes and walking away. The little bit of time (not spent in a laundromat!!!) lets you see the garment's condition. You can wash it gently if needed. I wipe out the washer tub after draining to get lint and hairs out so those don't get into the pump to keep it going.  Keeping the loads sorted, light weight, and balanced for the motors to last as long as possible. I think it is a good useful appliance.
